title: Health endpoint served at /api/books/health, not /health
evidence: src/main/java/com/bookapi/controller/BookController.java:78 maps @GetMapping("/health") under class base @RequestMapping("/api/books") (line 20), so it resolves to /api/books/health; a client calling GET /health per spec gets 404
suggestion: Expose the health check at the application root /health (a separate @RestController or Spring Actuator), not nested under the books collection

title: 4 controller integration tests fail — H2 IDENTITY sequence not reset across @Transactional rollback
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.7917 => 19/24 pass; BookControllerIntegrationTest asserts absolute ids/Location (line 51 header "/api/books/1", line 143 GET /api/books/1, line 178 PUT /api/books/1, line 199 DELETE /api/books/1) but @Transactional rolls back rows without resetting the auto-increment counter, so created books get ids > 1; agent's own _agent_stdout.log admits this
suggestion: Capture the created id from the POST response and use it in subsequent requests, or reset the schema per test (@DirtiesContext / recreate DDL), instead of hard-coding id=1

title: testBookValidationConstraints fails — validation not triggered on save under rollback
evidence: src/test/java/com/bookapi/repository/BookRepositoryTest.java:151 expects save() to throw ConstraintViolationException, but Hibernate bean validation fires on flush/commit which the @Transactional test rolls back, so no exception is thrown
suggestion: Call bookRepository.saveAndFlush(book) to force validation within the test, or validate via a jakarta Validator directly

title: Routes served under /api/books rather than spec paths /books
evidence: src/main/java/com/bookapi/controller/BookController.java:19 @RequestMapping("/api/books"); the spec (TASK.md) specifies POST /books, GET /books/{id}, etc. Behaviorally equivalent CRUD is present; noted for cross-run comparison
suggestion: If strict path conformance matters, map the controller at /books; otherwise accept the /api base as idiomatic

title: Mechanical scores read from scores.json (not re-run)
evidence: scores.json: code_quality=1.0, test_coverage=0.7917, defect_rate=1.0, maintainability=0.9617, idiomatic=0.7 — build+tests executed during scoring; JVM toolchain not re-invoked per evaluate-run guidance
